description: DECISIVE RISK: the whole SaaS-platform path assumes the headless agent-browser passes Cloudflare (OpenGov) and AWS WAF (PlanetBids). Only verified browser on OCWD/example.com (non-WAF). Test: Browser.inspect_page against a real OpenGov portal and a real PlanetBids bo-search; confirm it renders bid data, not a challenge page. If headless is also blocked, neither HTTP nor browser works -> must use official APIs. Blocks the OpenGov and PlanetBids coverage decisions.

comments.text: FINDING (tested live via GnomeGarden.Browser headless): OpenGov -> browser gets Cloudflare 'Just a moment... security verification' challenge page (title 'Just a moment...'), NOT bid data. PlanetBids -> WAF passes and the Ember shell renders (title 'PlanetBids Vendor Portal') but body innerText stays EMPTY after networkidle + 6s wait, 0 candidate rows, no login prompt -> the papi data fetch does not complete headless (bot-detection or auth). CONCLUSION: the headless browser is NOT a reliable path for these SaaS platforms. Implication: agency-site HTTP (gyl.1) is the viable channel; OpenGov/PlanetBids need official APIs or authenticated sessions (creds), not the headless browser. This reshapes gyl.4 (OpenGov = API/creds only) and gyl.5 (PlanetBids = creds/session or papi API; browser fallback is effectively non-functional).

title: Verify headless browser actually passes OpenGov/PlanetBids WAFs
